cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the given descriptions about Yves Gomes is true, with the options A through D. First, I need to read the passage carefully and check each option against the information provided. Starting with the first paragraph, Yves Gomes is a university student in Maryland who may have to leave the country. He wants to continue his education and go to medical school. He's an illegal immigrant who grew up in the US, fighting deportation to India, which he left with his parents at 14 months old. His parents were deported more than a year ago. Now let's go through each option: Option A: He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. The passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. That implies he was born in the US or somewhere else, but the key point is that he left India when he was very young. The parents were deported more than a year ago, which means they were in the US illegally. However, the passage doesn't explicitly state where he was born. But the fact that he's considered an illegal immigrant suggests he wasn't born in the US. Wait, but if he was brought to the US as a child, maybe he was born in India. Wait, he left India at 14 months, so he was born in India. But the parents were deported from the US. So if he was born in India, then he would have left India at 14 months with his parents who were going to the US. But the passage says he's an illegal immigrant, which would mean he wasn't born in the US. So Option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. That seems possible, but the passage doesn't explicitly say he was born in India. Wait, if he left India at 14 months, then he was born in India. So A might be correct. But wait, the passage says he left India when he was 14 months old, but doesn't explicitly say he was born there. Wait, how could he leave India at 14 months unless he was born there? That seems logical. So A might be correct. Let's hold that thought. Option B: His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago. The passage says his parents were deported more than a year ago. If they were deported from the US to India, then "forced to leave India" doesn't make sense. They were in the US illegally and were deported to India.
